target_audience.primary_persona: Enterprise integration developers and technical staff who need to build data synchronization workflows between business applications (CRM, ERP, accounting systems)
secondary_personas: System integrators connecting disparate business applications
SMB technical staff needing simplified integration solutions
pain_points: Difficulty connecting disparate business systems (Salesforce, Creatio, QuickBooks)
Complex API integration requiring custom code for each system
Need for real-time or scheduled data synchronization
Lack of visual tools for building integration workflows
High cost and complexity of enterprise integration platforms
goals: Create reliable data synchronization between business applications
Reduce manual data entry and copy-paste between systems
Automate business processes that span multiple applications
Build reusable integration flows without extensive coding
Deploy and manage integrations with minimal infrastructure
target_audience.usage_context: Used in enterprise/SMB environments to build, test, and deploy integration workflows. The IDE is used for development while the web portal handles authentication, configuration, and monitoring. Typically run on Windows workstations with database connectivity to shared or local servers.

product_vision.one_liner: Enterprise data integration platform for creating synchronization workflows between business applications
product_vision.problem_statement: Businesses use multiple applications (CRM, ERP, accounting, etc.) that don't natively communicate. Manual data transfer is error-prone and time-consuming. Existing integration platforms are expensive and complex. InterWeave IDE provides a visual, accessible approach to building integration flows.
product_vision.value_proposition: InterWeave IDE offers a simpler, more accessible alternative to enterprise integration platforms like MuleSoft or Boomi. It provides a visual Eclipse-based IDE with XML transformation capabilities, packaged as a portable Windows application with bundled runtime - no complex installation or infrastructure required.
success_metrics: Number of active integration projects created
Reduction in manual data entry time
User adoption rate (active users vs registered users)
Time to create a new integration workflow

existing_features: Eclipse-based IDE for building integration flows
Visual workflow designer for transaction flows, utility flows, and queries
XSLT transformation engine for XML-based data mapping
Apache Tomcat web portal with user authentication
MySQL database connectivity (Oracle Cloud, InterWeave server, offline modes)
Simple batch script deployment (START.bat, STOP.bat, CHANGE_DATABASE.bat)
known_gaps: Custom user authentication not working (only admin account functional)
No modern REST API for programmatic access
Missing automated testing framework for integration flows
No version control for integration projects
technical_debt: Proprietary LoginServlet with incompatible password hash format
Windows-specific batch scripts need Linux/Mac equivalents
Hardcoded admin credentials in authentication system
JSP-based web interface could be modernized

differentiators: Simpler deployment - portable Windows application with bundled runtime
Lower cost barrier for SMBs compared to enterprise solutions
XML/XSLT-based transformation approach familiar to enterprise developers
Visual Eclipse-based IDE rather than purely web-based
Self-hosted option for data privacy requirements
Offline capability for development without network access
competitive_context.market_position: Mid-market integration tool positioned between simple automation tools (Zapier) and complex enterprise platforms (MuleSoft). Best suited for SMBs and departmental use cases where a fully portable, self-contained integration solution is preferred over cloud-dependent SaaS platforms.

technical: Legacy Eclipse 3.1 architecture limits modern UI capabilities
Java 8 dependency (bundled) - should not upgrade without testing
Windows-first design requires significant work for cross-platform parity
Proprietary LoginServlet authentication requires decompilation to fix
XML/XSLT paradigm may be unfamiliar to newer developers
resources: Small development team (appears to be 1-2 contributors based on git history)
Limited bandwidth for major architectural changes
Documentation and training materials need updates
dependencies: Network connectivity for Oracle Cloud database mode
